stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (released ~April 25-26): record revenue ~Rs 2.98 lakh cr (+13-14% YoY) but consolidated net profit fell to ~Rs 16,971 cr (-8.8% to -12.5% YoY) on energy/O2C weakness. Board recommended final dividend of Rs 6/share. Management indicated Jio IPO likely delayed to H2 FY27 due to West Asia conflict. Jio 5G subscriber base reached 268 mn; fixed broadband market share ~43%. Stock ~Rs 1442.5 on May 4; market cap ~Rs 19.36 lakh cr.
stocks.sector_context: Energy/O2C segment dragged by crude price volatility and refining margin pressure; West Asia conflict driving crude surge. Digital and Retail segments remain bright spots. Crude surge and FII selling pressure cited as near-term market headwinds. Indian Oil & Gas index showing breakout strength.
stocks.peer_comparison: RIL dominant in O2C/Digital/Retail. Profit decline mirrors energy peers facing margin compression, but Jio + Retail growth is stronger than most diversified peers. BPCL trades cheaper (P/E ~9.6) but RIL has better growth optionality via digital/retail.
red_flags: Net profit declined 8.8-12.5% YoY in Q4 FY26 despite record revenue
Jio IPO timeline pushed out to H2 FY27 due to geopolitics
Crude price surge pressuring refining margins
Rupee depreciation 11% YoY pressuring import costs
US pressure forcing strategic shift away from Russian crude
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 6/share record date pending
Jio IPO (delayed but flagged by management)
AGM season approaching
Retail and Jio subscriber/ARPU traction
New Energy / giga-factory ramp

stocks.recent_news: Announced $11.75B all-cash acquisition of US-based Organon & Co on April 27, 2026 ($14/share). Stock jumped ~7% on announcement to ~Rs 1,733 (vs prior close Rs 1,620). Acquisition vaults Sun into top-25 global pharma with combined revenue ~$12.4B; adds women's health franchise and biosimilars portfolio (70+ products in 140 countries). Q4 FY26 earnings scheduled May 21, 2026.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD — sector outperforming as defensive on rupee depreciation and steady earnings. Indian pharma market grew 12.4% YoY in Feb 2026 (3rd consecutive double-digit month). US tariff overhang remains a sector risk.
stocks.peer_comparison: Sun is largest weight in Nifty Pharma at 23.79%. The Organon deal is a transformative inorganic move differentiating it from Dr Reddy's, Cipla, Lupin. 32 buy / 4 sell analyst ratings, consensus Buy with ~13% upside. Among recent top sector gainers.
red_flags: Large $11.75B all-cash deal raises leverage and integration risk
Organon's legacy generics franchise has been declining
US tariff overhang on Indian pharma exports
~7% pop on deal news may have priced in near-term upside ahead of Q4
catalysts: Q4 FY26 earnings on May 21, 2026
Organon acquisition closing milestones and synergy guidance
Specialty pharma pipeline (Ilumya, Cequa, Winlevi) updates
Sector rotation favoring defensives in volatile macro

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 + FY26 results announced April 30. Q4 consolidated revenue Rs 1,812 cr; Q4 net profit Rs 279 cr (+19% YoY). FY26 revenue Rs 6,813 cr (+23%); FY26 PAT Rs 889 cr (+~123%). FY26 EBITDA Rs 1,826 cr (+64%); EBITDA margin 26.8% vs 20.1% in FY25. CDMO revenue Rs 2,080 cr (+36% YoY) the primary driver. Second interim dividend Rs 1.20/share, record date May 8, 2026, payment on/after May 20. Two new independent directors (Dr Shekhar Mande, Sutapa Banerjee) effective July 2, 2026. Stock hit record high Rs 1,162.70 post-results.
stocks.sector_context: Pharma sector outperforming (Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD); pharma/healthcare cited as promising after extended corrective phase. Stabilising US generic pricing, robust domestic formulation growth, and complex generics pipeline supporting margins. CDMO/API benefiting from China+1 sourcing.
stocks.peer_comparison: Above-sector revenue/EBITDA growth. CDMO scale-up (Rs 2,080 cr, +36%) differentiates vs generics-heavy peers. KRKA JV for EU/APAC formulations provides regulated-market beachhead vs API-only peers. MOSL Buy with TP Rs 1,280 (~16% upside).
red_flags: Feb 2026 monthly export revenue fell 47% MoM to $36 mn (one-off vs trend unclear)
Trading near 52-week high — risk of profit-booking
Premium valuation at ~62x forward earnings / ~11.2x book leaves limited cushion
Rising FY27-28 capex intensity may weigh on near-term FCF
catalysts: Dividend Rs 1.20 record date May 8, 2026
MOSL Buy with TP Rs 1,280
CDMO momentum continuing (+36% in FY26)
KRKA JV ramp for EU/APAC formulations
Pharma sector rotation favoring quality names

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results released April 29: net profit Rs 534.14 cr (+68% YoY, +159.81% QoQ vs Rs 205.59 cr in Q3). Stock surged 12.44% to 52-week high Rs 200.89 post-results; up ~46% from 52-week low Rs 134.25 (Dec 9, 2025). NIM 6.2% with 30 bps QoQ expansion; guided +10-20 bps over next 2-3 quarters from term-deposit repricing. Credit cost down 149 bps QoQ to 1.9%. GNPA 3.27% (vs 3.33% QoQ); NNPA 0.97% (vs 0.99%). Loans Rs 1.54 trn (+12.6% YoY); deposits Rs 1.66 trn (+10% YoY). CAR 18.04%, Tier-1 17.3%. Rs 1.50 dividend recommended. JM Financial TP Rs 200 (ADD); Nuvama upgraded to HOLD with Rs 190 TP; CLSA also raised target. Promoter Bandhan Financial Holdings (39.74% stake) reportedly hiring i-bank to explore stake sale per RBI mandate to cut holding to 26% by 2030.
stocks.sector_context: Bank Nifty -2.19% on prior week; broader banking outlook cautious for week of May 4-8. Microfinance/SFB segment showing improving collections. Private banks like IDFC First, Bandhan, RBL offering top FD rates 7.25-7.40%. Bank credit costs normalizing post the MFI stress cycle of 2024-25.
stocks.peer_comparison: Strong post-results outperformer vs Bank Nifty weakness. NIM of 6.2% materially above large private peers. Asset-quality improvement differentiated within microfinance-heavy lenders. Stock now at 52-week high.
red_flags: GNPA still elevated at 3.27% (sequentially flat, not falling sharply)
MFI book (~40-45% of loans) remains monitorable due to West Bengal/Assam election cycle and El Nino risk
Stock now at 52-week high after 46% rally — risk of profit-booking
Promoter stake-sale overhang could create supply pressure
Broader Bank Nifty weakness could weigh near-term
catalysts: Multiple brokerage upgrades and target hikes (JM, Nuvama, CLSA)
Guided NIM expansion of further 10-20 bps over next 2-3 quarters
Continued credit-cost normalization as MFI slippages decline
Improving collection efficiency (98.9% in March vs 98.1% in December)
Promoter stake sale could attract a strategic acquirer and re-rate the stock
Rs 1.50 dividend payout

stocks.recent_news: Board meeting scheduled May 11, 2026 to consider Q4 FY26 audited results and final FY26 dividend. Set up Singapore cloud subsidiary on April 29, 2026, eyeing global data centre expansion. On April 30, acquired 12,500 shares making Romano Projects a wholly-owned subsidiary. Signed supplementary agreement with Destination Properties on a Gurugram residential project. Stock ~Rs 508.90-510 on May 4; market cap ~Rs 17,522 cr; 52-week high Rs 743.65, low Rs 403. Q3 FY26: revenue +20% YoY to Rs 641.59 cr, PBT +30% YoY to Rs 171.78 cr. Data centre business generated ~Rs 58.42 cr in H1 FY26 with 28 MW operational; target 63 MW by Dec 2026, 117 MW by FY28. Rs 4,500 cr MoU with Andhra Pradesh govt for new data centres.
stocks.sector_context: BSE Realty index +17.37% past month vs Sensex +4.61%; sector seeing momentum. Industry value growth forecast 10-15% in 2026 with ~70% of developers expecting >5% price rises. Headwinds: construction costs +3-5% on labour wage hikes from Nov 2025 labour laws. Data centre sub-segment a structural growth story benefiting from Budget 2026 incentives and AI-led demand.
stocks.peer_comparison: Strongest 1-year peer return (~12.08%) supported by earnings growth and conservative balance sheet. However, +8.39% past month lags BSE Realty +17.37% — short-term underperformer despite strong fundamentals. Data centre pivot differentiates it from pure-play realty peers (DLF, Godrej, Oberoi).
red_flags: Trailed sector index over the last month despite strong fundamentals
Stock ~31% off 52-week high of Rs 743.65 — momentum has faded
Rising construction costs (+3-5%) industry-wide pressure on margins
Capex-heavy data-centre roadmap may pressure cash flows
Execution risk on AP Rs 4,500 cr MoU and Singapore expansion
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results and final dividend on May 11, 2026 (event-driven)
Data centre capacity ramp (28 MW to 63 MW by Dec 2026, 117 MW by FY28)
Ashok Cloud services nearing operationalization at Manesar/Panchkula
Singapore cloud subsidiary launch
Rs 4,500 cr Andhra Pradesh data centre MoU progressing
Rent-free period expiries lifting DC rentals into FY27
